🗺️ Get Started

How to Get Involved

Four steps. Less than 5 minutes. Completely free.

01
Find Your City Chapter
Search the table below. Join the Instagram, Facebook and WhatsApp — each serves a different purpose.
2 min
02
Join National Community
Follow @degisnetwork and join the National Facebook Group for Germany-wide updates.
1 min
03
Attend a Local Event
City chapters run regular meetups, language exchanges and networking events. Check your city's IG.
Anytime
04
Become a Veteran
Been in Germany 6+ months? Pay it forward — earn an official volunteer certificate for your CV.
Give back
+
City Not Listed?
Become the FOUNDER of DEGIS in your city. You'll be building something that matters for thousands of students.
Start a chapter →

👋 The person behind this portfolio

Hi, I'm Noopur —
call me Noor.

Master's student at ESCP Business School (Paris & Berlin) · International student in Berlin · Independent creator · 3+ years building marketing strategy and social media growth across international environments.

I don't just understand DEGIS's audience — I am the audience. As an international student navigating Germany myself, everything in this portfolio comes from lived experience of what Newbies actually need.

"I don't just understand DEGIS's audience — I am the audience."
As an international student in Berlin myself, I've navigated the Anmeldung, the housing hunt, the bureaucracy, the loneliness of starting fresh. Everything in this portfolio is built from that lived experience — not guesswork.

02 · Newsletter
DEGIS Housing Dispatch — Issue 01
Biweekly email newsletter + gated PDF lead magnet. Students submit email to download the Anti-Scam Housing Guide. Promoted via IG Stories and TikTok.
Issue 01 · September 2025
🏠 DEGIS Housing Dispatch
The no-BS housing guide for international students in Germany
🚨 Scam Watch
The "Pay 3 Months Before Viewing" trick is spreading.
German law: no landlord can demand more than 3 months Kaltmiete as Kaution, and never before a contract is signed.
💬 Veteran Voice
"I slept on a friend's couch for 3 weeks because I didn't know you could request viewings through ImmobilienScout in advance." — DEGIS Berlin Veteran
03 · Social Campaign
"Red Flags" — 6-Slide Carousel
Swipeable carousel optimised for saves. Each slide = one housing red flag. Drives saves (IG's highest-value signal) and peer sharing.
1
DEGIS Housing Guide
Housing Scams in Germany: What No One Warns You About 🏚️
Seven red flags every international student must know before signing anything.
Swipe to learn →
2
Red Flag #1
🚩 "Pay first, view later."
No legitimate landlord in Germany asks for money before you've seen the flat in person. Always a scam.
3
Red Flag #2
🚩 "The landlord is abroad."
They claim to be travelling and will "send the keys by mail after payment." German law requires in-person Übergabe.
4
Red Flag #3
🚩 Deposit over 3 months Kaltmiete.
German law (§551 BGB) caps the security deposit at 3 months cold rent. More = uninformed or dishonest.
5
DEGIS can help 💪
You don't have to figure this out alone.
DEGIS Veterans have been there. The Buddy Program matches you with someone who survived the housing hunt. Completely free.
Join the Buddy Program →
6
Save + Share
Share this with every Newbie you know. 🙏
Tag a friend moving to Germany. Follow @degisnetwork for more survival guides.
@degisnetwork
04 · Chapter Enablement
First 30 Days: Chapter Marketing VP Cheat Sheet
A plug-and-play guide for new chapter Marketing VPs. Lives in a shared Google Drive / Notion hub. Removes the "where do I even start" barrier for 90+ city chapters.
📅 Week 1 — Set Up (Days 1–7)
✓ Access the DEGIS Canva Brand Kit · ✓ Set up city Instagram with official bio template · ✓ Post first event with the 7-day countdown template
📊 3 KPIs You Track Every Month
① Instagram Reach (target: +15% month-on-month) · ② Event sign-up clicks from social · ③ New follower growth (target: +50 per month)
🗓️ The Weekly Posting Rhythm
Monday: Event announcement · Wednesday: Tip or student story · Friday: Event reminder · Sunday: Photo dump Reel
05 · B2B Sponsorship
Sponsorship One-Pager: Partner with DEGIS
Target: German companies seeking visibility with international STEM talent — Expatrio, Fintiba, student health insurers, co-working spaces.

06 · Content Calendar
September Arrival Calendar — Multi-Channel
Mapped to the student arrival journey: Week 1 = chaos, Week 2 = bureaucracy, Week 3 = find your people, Week 4 = get involved. Each post has a platform, format, and single CTA.

07 · Creator Strategy
City Takeover Series: Micro-Influencer Strategy
1–2 student content creators per city (2K–15K followers) for a rotating "takeover" series. Zero budget — exchange is DEGIS visibility, event access, and co-branding.
DELIVERABLES
• 3 IG Stories/day × 7 days
• 1 Reel (60–90s) at week's end
• 1 TikTok duet with @degisnetwork
CREATOR GETS
• Feature on @degisnetwork (41K)
• Free event access for the month
• LinkedIn recommendation
🎬 "POV: First week in Germany and I know nothing"
🎬 "Things I wish I knew before moving to Berlin 🇩🇪"
🎬 "I asked 10 international students what their anchor in Germany is…"
🎬 "Germany made me a morning person (here's the proof)"
08 · Content Series
"Phrase of the Week" — TikTok Series (52 episodes)
Weekly 30-second TikTok + Reel. Each teaches one German phrase international students actually need. 52 episodes = one year of consistent, searchable content.
[EPISODE 12] — Reschedule Bürgeramt [0:00] "You got a Bürgeramt appointment for 3 months away... and you can't make it." [0:05] The phrase: "Ich würde gerne meinen Termin verschieben." → "I would like to reschedule my appointment." [0:25] "Follow for next week: telling your landlord the heating is broken. 🥶" # #LearnGerman #InternationalStudent #GermanLife
Ep 01
"Ich möchte mich anmelden"
Ep 02
"Haben Sie einen Termin?"
Ep 12
"Termin verschieben"
Ep 24
"Die Heizung funktioniert nicht"
09 · Policy → Content
PIE Data → 5-Slide LinkedIn Carousel
Turn the Stifterverband × DEGIS research paper into a scroll-stopping LinkedIn carousel. Two versions: B2C for students, B2B for university International Offices.
1
DEGIS × Stifterverband Research
3×
More likely to get hired in Germany.
International students who graduate from a German university are 3× more likely to find employment.
Swipe →
2
The Scale
1.85M
STEM workers leaving Germany in 10 years.
Germany's demographic crisis is already here. International students are one of the fastest ways to close this gap.
3
The #1 Success Factor
84%
Higher employment with field-related work.
Practical experience — not just grades — is what German employers look for.
4
The DEGIS Solution
Community is the missing infrastructure.
DEGIS connects 41,000+ international students with Veterans, events, and language practice across 51 German cities.
Partner with DEGIS →
5
Read the full research
Between Graduation and Career Entry.
Published by Stifterverband and DEGIS. Download at degis.info/policy-lab.
@degisnetwork
